description: Replace page numbers with industry-standard scene length measurements in eighths (1/8ths of a page) across all reports and components

subtasks.description: Create database migration 024_add_scene_length_eighths.sql to add page_length_eighths column with index
Implement calculation functions: calculate_eighths_from_content() (primary), calculate_eighths_from_pages() (fallback), and format_eighths() helper
Create backfill script scripts/backfill_scene_eighths.py to calculate eighths for all existing scenes
Update AI extraction in analysis_worker.py and scene_enhancer.py to calculate and store page_length_eighths during scene analysis
Update report_service.py: modify aggregate_scene_data(), _render_scene_breakdown(), _render_one_liner(), and all 8 department reports to use eighths
Update frontend components: SceneDetail.jsx, Stripboard.jsx with formatEighths() utility and proper display
Run database migration and backfill script on development database
Test all 9 report types (Scene Breakdown, One-Liner, 8 department reports) for correct eighths display and PDF export
Test frontend components, edge cases (missing data, very short/long scenes, omitted scenes), and end-to-end workflow
Production deployment: run migration, backfill production data, monitor for issues

recommended_approach: Option B (content-based calculation) as primary method with Option A (page-range) as fallback
key_benefits: Industry-standard measurement (1/8 page ≈ 1 minute screen time)
More accurate production scheduling and budgeting
Professional report appearance matching industry expectations
